scouting_report: Erratic Wide Disruptor - Wide players (RM/LM) who carry into the penalty area and tackle in the middle third, but are prone to errors and have poor short passing.
Combative Midfielder - Central defensive midfielders heavily involved in challenges (won and lost) with decent set-piece/penalty xG, but lacking open-play progressive passing.
Volume Tackler - Midfielders who engage in a massive volume of defensive challenges and pass attempts, but provide virtually zero direct goals or assists.
Direct Target Forward - Strikers who receive progressive passes and carry the ball forward to generate goals/assists, but rarely attempt 1v1 dribbles or long passes.
Safe Circulator CB - Center-backs who dominate in short passing and total distance of passes, but avoid progressive ball carrying, expected goal threat, and active defending.
Deep-Lying Dribbler - Deep midfielders who excel at escaping pressure via dribbling and hitting long passes, but offer almost zero direct attacking output (goals/assists).
Aggressive Wide Carrier - Wide midfielders who press high and carry the ball into the box, but are prone to miscontrols and lack final-third end-product.
Fiery Playmaker - Central midfielders who generate high expected assists and shot-on-target efficiency, but play extremely aggressively, leading to a high rate of red cards.
Set-Piece Defender - Defenders playing heavy minutes with high expected attacking output from set-pieces (free kicks), but lacking deep defensive tackling and ball-carrying.
Direct Attacking Midfielder - High-volume shooters and progressive passers who also take penalties. They play high-risk, direct football rather than safe short combinations.
Overlapping CB Anomaly - A strange center-back profile that attempts crosses into the penalty area but gets dispossessed often. Extremely low traditional progressive carrying.
Efficient Finisher - Attackers who significantly overperform their xG and play heavy minutes, but have remarkably low involvement in passing or receiving progressive passes.
Progressive Forward - Strikers and attackers who take high-quality shots and carry the ball progressively, but actively avoid 1v1 dribbles and long passes.
Possession Fullback - Fullbacks (and penalty takers) who act as possession hubs with massive passing volume and accuracy, but offer minimal direct goal/assist threat.
Long-Passing Mid/Back - Midfielders or fullbacks who progress the ball efficiently via long passes, but rarely carry the ball directly into the final third.
Deep Playmaker CB - Center-backs highly involved in attacking play, registering assists and passes into the final 3rd, while ignoring traditional defensive interventions.
Wide Playmaker - Wide players who generate elite expected assists/goals and make progressive passes, but lack 1v1 dribbling success and total passing volume.
Complete Target - Elite center-forwards dominating non-penalty goals and assists. They constantly receive progressive passes in dangerous areas without needing to drop deep.
Defensive Fullback - Fullbacks with high passing accuracy and shot-blocking numbers (plus some penalty duties), but absolute zero offensive shooting threat.
Advanced Engine - Central midfielders playing heavy minutes with high expected non-penalty goal/assist threat and long passing, but they lack deep defensive tackling.
